name: win32_event_log
explanation: This is the Windows Event Viewer integration, distinct from the `win_event_log` Datadog Agent (Go) check of the same family. The Python check here is `Win32EventLogCheck(AgentCheck, ConfigMixin)` and reads the local Event Log via the `win32evtlog` pywin32 API (EvtSubscribe, EvtRender, etc.). The default `server: localhost` means no remote credentials are needed; the `user`/`password`/`domain`/`auth_type` fields are only consulted when `server` is set to a remote machine. The required `path` field is the Event Log channel name (e.g. `System`, `Application`, `Security`) — a fixed enumeration of well-known Windows channels — so an auto-config layer running on a Windows host can deterministically populate one instance per standard channel, the same way the existing IIS / windows_service integrations populate from local OS state. Discovery is `is this a Windows host?` rather than a network probe, so method is `other`.
notes: Pattern H (local Windows host detection). The required `path` is a Windows channel name from a small well-known set; auto-config can pre-populate `System`/`Application` (and `Security` when legacy_mode is on) without further user input. Remote-server mode is opt-in and would fall through to credentials-required, but it's not the default. Note: this is the legacy/v2-Python check; there is also a separate `win_event_log` core Agent check that ships logs via `type: windows_event` — they are different integration tiles.
